claim: The Khmer Rouge's sacrificial-escalation mechanism is contested between a collective-immortality-to-sacrifice reading (the Angkar as sacred collective body) and an agrarian-utopian reading in which sacrifice was instrumentalized toward ideological goals rather than rooted in order sacralization per se.
rationale: The distinction matters for theory-testing: if Khmer Rouge violence was primarily instrumental (eliminating class enemies for material goals) rather than symbolic (sacrifice for collective immortality), it may test a different causal pathway than the primary theory predicts.
